_comment: National standards overlay. ONLY Florida is fully populated (status=populated, verified codes + adapter + corpus). Every other state is a STUB with room to fill in: set its status to 'populated', fill subjects[*].framework/source/example, set codes_verified, and add adapter + resources_dir. See state-standards-map.md 'Populating a state'. Verified June 2026.
populate_checklist: Confirm the state's frameworks on its DOE (and CCSS/NGSS status).
Create shared/standards/<state>-best.md with VERIFIED coding schemes (mirror florida-best.md).
Optionally add resources/<state>/ corpus + sources.json (mirror florida/).
In states.json: set status=populated, fill subjects[*], set adapter + resources_dir.
Never invent codes — verify on the DOE (protocols/standards-verification.md).

states.name: Arizona
states.note: adopted then repealed/replaced CCSS (state set is CCSS-influenced)

states.name: Florida

computer_science.notes: FL moving CS to a standalone framework (2025+); verify on CPALMS
